title: alchemilla vulgaris var. compta
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris var. compta, also known as the lady's mantle, is a plant species within the Rosaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to Europe, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and astringent effects. The lady's mantle has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including menstrual cramps, digestive problems, and wounds, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Herbal preparations of A. vulgaris var. compta include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of menopause, reduce bleeding, and soothe digestive issues.
